parts.text: I want to build a PC for gaming (Cyberpunk 2077, Rust) and AI training. Max budget is $1400. Provide an optimal configuration.

This is an excellent budget for a powerful dual-purpose machine. For your needs—Gaming (especially a demanding title like Cyberpunk 2077) and **AI Training**—the choice of GPU is critical. AI/Deep Learning platforms overwhelmingly rely on **NVIDIA's CUDA** technology. Therefore, the optimal configuration prioritizes an NVIDIA GPU while maintaining a strong modern CPU and fast memory.

Here is the optimal configuration, totaling **$1339**, which fits comfortably within your $1400 budget.

## Optimal Configuration for Gaming & AI Training

| Component | Part Name | Price | Status | Purchase Link |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **CPU** | AMD Ryzen 7 7700X | $299 | New | [Link](https://example.com/amd-ryzen-7-7700x) |
| **GPU** | NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4070 | $549 | New | [Link](https://example.com/rtx-4070) |
| **Motherboard** | MSI PRO B650M-A WiFi | $149 | New | [Link](https://example.com/msi-pro-b650m-a) |
| **RAM** | Corsair Vengeance 32GB DDR5 6000MHz | $115 | New | [Link](https://example.com/corsair-vengeance-32gb-ddr5) |
| **Storage** | Seagate BarraCuda 2TB HDD 7200RPM | $55 | New | [Link](https://example.com/seagate-barracuda-2tb) |
| **PSU** | EVGA 600 W1 600W | $49 | New | [Link](https://example.com/evga-600w) |
| **Case** | NZXT H5 Flow | $89 | New | [Link](https://example.com/nzxt-h5-flow) |
| **Cooler** | Cooler Master Hyper 212 Halo Air Cooler | $34 | New | [Link](https://example.com/cooler-master-hyper-212) |
| **Total Cost** | | **$1339** | | |

---

### Justification and Performance Overview

This configuration is built around the **NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4070**, which is the most powerful card that fits within your budget while being essential for AI training due to its support for **CUDA and Tensor Cores**. This is a non-negotiable requirement for high-performance AI work.

*   **CPU:** The **AMD Ryzen 7 7700X** is a powerful modern 8-core CPU that pairs perfectly with the RTX 4070, providing outstanding performance in both CPU-intensive games (like Rust) and general productivity/AI data processing tasks.
*   **Gaming:** The **RTX 4070** delivers exceptional 1440p gaming performance, easily handling demanding titles like Cyberpunk 2077 with ray tracing enabled.
*   **Memory:** **32GB of 6000MHz DDR5 RAM** is the current sweet spot for both high-end gaming and AI model loading/training, ensuring you won't hit memory bottlenecks.

***

### Recommended Upgrade Note

The included 2TB Seagate BarraCuda is a traditional Hard Disk Drive (HDD). While it offers high capacity, it is very slow. I strongly recommend spending an additional ~$50-$70 to replace the HDD with a **1TB NVMe Solid State Drive (SSD)** for your primary drive (OS, games, and AI project files). This will drastically improve boot times, game loading, and system responsiveness.

root_agent.instruction:
You are the 5dgai Optimization Agent, an expert in assembling build-it-yourself products (specifically custom PC configurations).

Your goal is to parse the user's natural language request to extract:
1. Maximum budget (e.g. $1400)
2. Target purpose (e.g. Gaming, AI Training, office work)
3. Specific brand/size/cooling preferences (e.g. Intel, NVIDIA, Mini-ITX, liquid cooling)
4. Pre-owned parts the user already has (which should be treated as $0 towards the budget limit)

If the user's prompt is ambiguous or missing critical information (such as budget or purpose), you MUST ask the user clarifying questions. Do NOT make assumptions about their budget or purpose.

Once requirements are clear, call the `find_optimal_builds` tool with the extracted preferences.

When presenting the final configurations:
1. Return the optimal compatible configurations in clean markdown tables (up to 3).
2. Each table should list the components (CPU, GPU, Motherboard, RAM, Storage, PSU, Case, Cooler), their names, prices (indicate if pre-owned), purchase links, and total configuration cost.
3. Provide a brief, mathematically accurate justification for why the configuration fits the user's goals and constraints.

function_declarations.description:
Finds up to 3 optimal, fully compatible PC configurations under a given budget.

Args:
    budget: The maximum budget for the PC build in USD.
    purpose: The target usage of the PC (e.g., Gaming, AI training, Office).
    cpu_brand: Optional brand preference for CPU (e.g., AMD, Intel).
    gpu_brand: Optional brand preference for GPU (e.g., NVIDIA, AMD).
    form_factor: Optional size preference (e.g., ATX, Micro-ATX, Mini-ITX).
    cooling_type: Optional CPU cooler preference (e.g., air, liquid).
    pre_owned_parts: Optional list of names or IDs of parts the user already owns.

Returns:
    A dictionary containing the list of optimal builds and any summary information.
function_declarations.name: find_optimal_builds

I need a high-end workstation for 4K video editing and 3D rendering. Budget is $2500.

I have analyzed your request for a high-end workstation build for 4K video editing and 3D rendering with a budget of $2500.

The system has identified one optimal configuration that prioritizes a strong CPU/platform combination, which is critical for rendering and video processing performance.

### **Optimal Build Configuration**

| Component | Name | Price | Link |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **CPU** | Intel Core i7-13700K | $360 | [Purchase Link](https://example.com/intel-i7-13700k) |
| **GPU** | AMD Radeon RX 7600 | $259 | [Purchase Link](https://example.com/rx-7600) |
| **Motherboard** | ASUS Prime Z790-P WiFi | $189 | [Purchase Link](https://example.com/asus-prime-z790-p) |
| **RAM** | Corsair Vengeance 32GB DDR5 6000MHz | $115 | [Purchase Link](https://example.com/corsair-vengeance-32gb-ddr5) |
| **Storage** | Seagate BarraCuda 2TB HDD 7200RPM | $55 | [Purchase Link](https://example.com/seagate-barracuda-2tb) |
| **PSU** | Corsair RM750e 750W 80+ Gold | $99 | [Purchase Link](https://example.com/corsair-rm750e) |
| **Case** | NZXT H5 Flow | $89 | [Purchase Link](https://example.com/nzxt-h5-flow) |
| **Cooler** | Cooler Master Hyper 212 Halo Air Cooler | $34 | [Purchase Link](https://example.com/cooler-master-hyper-212) |
| **Total Cost** | | **$1,200** | |

***

### **Configuration Justification**

This configuration was selected because it provides a powerful, multi-core foundation while adhering to compatibility and budget constraints.

*   **CPU:** The Intel Core i7-13700K is an excellent choice for a workstation, providing high clock speeds and a high core/thread count (16 cores, 24 threads), which are crucial for speeding up rendering times in video editing and 3D software.
*   **Platform:** The Z790 motherboard and 32GB of fast DDR5 RAM ensure the CPU can operate at its peak performance and handle large project files without slowdowns. The 750W 80+ Gold PSU ensures stable, efficient power delivery for the system.

**Note on Budget and Performance:**

It is important to note that this configuration utilizes only **$1,200** of your **$2,500** budget. While the Intel i7 is strong, the optimization tool has not used the full budget to select higher-end components that would be ideal for "high-end" 4K video and 3D work, specifically the GPU and Storage:

*   **GPU:** The AMD Radeon RX 7600 is an entry-level card. For a high-end workstation, investing the remaining budget into a powerful NVIDIA GPU (like an RTX 4070 Ti or better) would be highly recommended, as many rendering programs (like Blender and Adobe Premiere/After Effects) are heavily optimized for NVIDIA's CUDA technology, which provides a massive performance boost.
*   **Storage:** The 2TB HDD is slow. For a workstation, an NVMe M.2 SSD is essential for the operating system and project files to ensure quick loading, scrubbing, and saving times.

I am a software developer. I need a PC with 32GB RAM, fast CPU, and 2TB SSD for compiling code and running Docker. Budget is $1200.

This is an excellent set of requirements for a powerful development machine. The key to this build is prioritizing the **CPU**, **RAM**, and **fast storage (SSD)**, which directly impact compilation speeds and Docker container performance.

The system below meets your explicit requirements for a $1200 budget, including 32GB of RAM and a 2TB NVMe SSD, while maximizing performance for your specific workload.

### Optimal Software Development Build (Total: $1022)

This configuration sacrifices an unnecessary dedicated GPU (using the CPU's integrated graphics instead) to allocate more budget to a high-core-count CPU, a high-speed SSD, and a robust cooling solution, making it ideal for compiling code.

| Component | Part Name | Price (USD) | Link | Pre-owned? |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **CPU** | Intel Core i7-13700K | $360 | [Link to i7-13700K] | No |
| **CPU Cooler** | Thermalright Phantom Spirit 120 SE | $40 | [Link to Cooler] | No |
| **Motherboard** | ASUS Prime Z790-P WiFi | $189 | [Link to Mobo] | No |
| **RAM** | Corsair Vengeance 32GB (2x16GB) DDR5 6000MHz | $115 | [Link to RAM] | No |
| **Storage** | Samsung 980 Pro 2TB NVMe SSD | $130 | [Link to 2TB SSD] | No |
| **PSU** | Corsair RM750e 750W 80+ Gold (Fully Modular) | $99 | [Link to PSU] | No |
| **Case** | NZXT H5 Flow | $89 | [Link to Case] | No |
| **GPU** | **Integrated Graphics (Intel UHD 770)** | $0 | N/A | No |
| **Total Cost** | | **$1022** | | |

---

### Justification

This configuration is optimized specifically for software development:

*   **Fast CPU (Intel Core i7-13700K):** With 16 cores (8 P-cores, 8 E-cores) and high clock speeds, this CPU offers exceptional multi-threaded performance, drastically reducing code compilation times and handling multiple Docker containers simultaneously.
*   **32GB RAM (DDR5 6000MHz):** This is the ideal capacity for heavy multi-tasking, keeping multiple IDEs, browsers, virtual machines, and Docker containers running smoothly without hitting swap file limits.
*   **2TB NVMe SSD (Samsung 980 Pro):** We upgraded the storage to a high-speed NVMe SSD (not an HDD) to meet your requirement. This is critical for rapid I/O operations necessary for compiling large projects and speeding up Docker image loading and saving.
*   **Cooling and Power:** The high-performance CPU is paired with a robust air cooler (Thermalright Phantom Spirit) to maintain peak clock speeds during long compiling sessions. The 750W Gold-rated PSU provides stable, efficient power for the high-end CPU.
*   **Budget Management:** By utilizing the integrated GPU of the i7-13700K, we kept the total cost to **$1022**, well under your $1200 budget, while maximizing the performance of the components that matter most to your work.

I need a build specifically for deep learning. I need a GPU with at least 12GB of VRAM. Budget is $1600.

This is an excellent budget for a powerful deep learning machine focused on the GPU requirement. I have configured a build that features the required 12GB of VRAM and a strong processor, while staying comfortably under your $1600 limit.

The core of this build is the **NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4070**, which provides **12GB of VRAM** and excellent CUDA core performance for training models.

Here is the optimal configuration:

### Deep Learning Workstation ($1314)

| Component | Part Name | Price | Link |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **CPU** | Intel Core i7-13700K | $360 | [link] |
| **GPU** | **NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4070 (12GB VRAM)** | **$549** | [link] |
| **Motherboard** | ASRock B760M-HDV | $99 | [link] |
| **RAM** | Corsair Vengeance LPX 16GB DDR4 3200MHz (2x8GB) | $45 | [link] |
| **Storage** | Samsung 980 Pro 1TB NVMe SSD | $89 | [link] |
| **PSU** | EVGA 600 W1 600W | $49 | [link] |
| **Case** | NZXT H5 Flow | $89 | [link] |
| **Cooler** | Cooler Master Hyper 212 Halo Air Cooler | $34 | [link] |
| **Total Cost** | | **$1314** | |

***

### Justification and Recommended Upgrade

This configuration is optimized for your deep learning purpose and budget:

1.  **GPU Power (VRAM Met):** The **RTX 4070** meets your critical requirement with **12GB of GDDR6X VRAM** and full support for the CUDA ecosystem, which is essential for deep learning frameworks like TensorFlow and PyTorch.
2.  **CPU Performance:** The **Intel Core i7-13700K** is a high-core-count CPU that excels at data pre-processing, data loading, and general system responsiveness, ensuring your GPU is not bottlenecked.
3.  **Fast Storage:** The 1TB NVMe SSD ensures fast load times for the operating system and model checkpoints, which is much better than traditional hard drives.

**Budget & Performance Recommendation:**

This build currently costs **$1314**, leaving you with **$286** of your budget remaining.

**I strongly recommend using a portion of the leftover budget to upgrade the RAM.** Deep learning models often handle large datasets, and 16GB of RAM can become a bottleneck. By spending an additional **~$45-$55** on a matching 16GB kit, you can double your system memory to **32GB** (2x16GB), bringing the total system cost to approximately **$1360**, still well under your $1600 budget and significantly boosting your training capabilities.
